title: Zanthoxylum prancei
taxonomy: Zanthoxylum prancei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family consists of approximately 160 genera and 1,000 species of plants. Within the Rutaceae family, Zanthoxylum prancei is part of the genus Zanthoxylum, which comprises around 230 species of plants. The genus Zanthoxylum is further divided into several subgenera and sections, and Zanthoxylum prancei is one of the species within this complex classification system. Further study is needed to determine the exact classification and relationships within the Zanthoxylum genus.

Its leaves and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other conditions. intro: Zanthoxylum prancei, commonly known as the Amazonian prickly ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to provide relief from pain, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum prancei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
